gdp_real_growth_pct.trend_reasoning: War entering third week with 6,000+ targets struck. Infrastructure destruction accelerating. Hormuz mined, halting oil exports (~70% of revenue). New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei defiant. GDP trajectory: deep contraction -15% to -25% annualized.

inflation_cpi_pct.trend_reasoning: Hyperinflation accelerating. War entering third week. Currency collapse, supply chains destroyed, panic hoarding. New Supreme Leader's defiance ensures continued economic collapse.

current_account_pct_gdp.trend_reasoning: Oil export revenue eliminated by Hormuz mine-laying and US bombing. Current account swinging deeply negative. Import costs surging via overland routes.

govt_debt_pct_gdp.trend_reasoning: Iran debt-to-GDP ratio exploding as GDP collapses and emergency war spending surges. Nominal debt metrics meaningless given currency collapse.

sovereign_credit_rating.value: S&P: NR, Moody's: NR, Fitch: B+

central_bank_policy_rate_pct.trend_reasoning: Iran central bank policy rate irrelevant in war conditions. Monetary policy transmission destroyed.

fdi_inflows_usd.trend_reasoning: Iran FDI flows halted by active war. International investors withdrawing. Capital flight accelerating. No new investment possible under current conditions.

exchange_rate_vs_usd.trend_reasoning: Iran rial in freefall. War, Hormuz closure, export halt driving currency collapse. Parallel market diverging from official rate.

sovereign_bond_yield_10yr_pct.trend_reasoning: Iran sovereign bonds effectively untradeable. Default risk near certainty. No functioning bond market during active war.

military_expenditure_pct_gdp.trend_reasoning: Iran military expenditure as % GDP surging as GDP collapses and war spending maximized. Total mobilization underway.

composite_national_power_index.trend_reasoning: Iran national power collapsing. Military infrastructure being destroyed. Economy in freefall. Supreme Leader killed and replaced. International isolation deepening.

political_risk_premium_bps.methodology: Sovereign rating implied spread + adjustments for instability, conflict, sanctions, autocracy

overall_investment_risk_score.trend_reasoning: Iran investment risk at maximum. Active war, economy collapsed, assets frozen, Hormuz mined. No investable opportunities.

resource_self_sufficiency_index.methodology: Average of min(production/consumption, 1.0) for energy, food, water, minerals

supply_chain_chokepoint_exposure.methodology: 0.6 * maritime_chokepoint_dependency + 0.4 * normalized_trade_openness; elevated due to active Hormuz/Suez disruption

market_accessibility_score.methodology: Average of capital_account_openness, stock_market_cap/200, financial_dev_index, ease_of_business/100

trade_openness_trend.reasoning: Iran trade openness collapsing as Hormuz disruption blocks maritime trade. Both exports and imports severely disrupted. Overland routes insufficient to replace sea trade.

top_partner_relationship_health.reasoning: Iran's key bilateral relationships in crisis. At war with USA/Israel. Russia providing covert support but relationship asymmetric. China maintaining distance. No major partner providing effective assistance.

political_stability_trend.reasoning: Iran political stability collapsing. Former Supreme Leader killed. New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei installed under extreme duress. Active war with foreign powers. 1,348 civilians killed, 17,000+ injured. Regime survival in question.

narrative.executive_summary: Iran is in a state of wartime economic and political collapse. The US-Israeli war enters its third week with 6,000+ targets struck and 1,348 civilians killed. Mojtaba Khamenei has assumed power as Supreme Leader after his father was killed on February 28. The Strait of Hormuz remains mined. Iran's economy is in freefall with GDP contraction estimated at -15% to -20% annualized. Investment risk is at maximum (0.791) with the political risk premium at 4,965 bps. Infrastructure destruction is accelerating. Kurdish ground invasion opens a new front from the west.
key_changes_this_week: War enters third week — 6,000+ targets struck, 1,348 civilians killed, 17,000+ injured
Mojtaba Khamenei assumes Supreme Leader role after father killed — vows continued resistance
Hormuz remains mined — new Supreme Leader vows to maintain blockade
Trump says US military 'totally obliterated' targets on Kharg Island oil terminal
Iran-made drones strike Cyprus — EU member drawn into conflict
Kurdish ground invasion continues from the west
narrative.outlook: Iran faces existential economic and political crisis. No ceasefire in sight. Infrastructure destruction accelerating with Kharg Island oil terminal struck. Mojtaba Khamenei's leadership untested — IRGC effective power holders. Hormuz mining continues, deepening global energy disruption. GDP contraction among worst in modern Middle Eastern history. Regime change scenario is non-trivial.
narrative.investor_implications: Iran is entirely uninvestable. All accessible assets effectively frozen. No capital market access. Sanctions regime intensifying. Currency worthless for investment purposes. Bonds in default territory. The only investable angle is indirect — through oil price plays and regional contagion hedges. Duration and outcome of war is the single determinant of any future investability.
narrative.data_quality_note: Data quality severely impaired by wartime conditions. Internet shutdown limits information flow. All forward-looking estimates carry extreme uncertainty. Investment risk at maximum critical severity.

name: Iran
executive_summary: Iran is in the most severe crisis since the 1979 revolution. US-Israeli Operation Epic Fury struck 1,000+ targets on Feb 28, killing Supreme Leader Khamenei. IRGC retaliated against 27 US bases and Gulf states on Mar 1, closing the Strait of Hormuz. The economy faces collapse: GDP strong_decrease, inflation at 42.4% approaching hyperinflation, currency in freefall, trade completely disrupted. Political succession crisis with no clear heir during 40-day mourning. Iran is effectively uninvestable across all asset classes.
key_changes: US-Israeli strikes hit 1,000+ targets; Supreme Leader Khamenei killed (Feb 28, transformative)
IRGC retaliated against 27 US bases and Gulf states; Strait of Hormuz closed (Mar 1, transformative)
Succession crisis with no clear heir; IRGC consolidating control during mourning period
Inflation at 42.4% approaching hyperinflation threshold; currency collapse accelerating
Strong_decrease trends across GDP, trade, FDI, current account, political stability
outlook: Iran faces state collapse risk in the near-to-medium term. IRGC control may stabilize security apparatus but cannot address economic collapse. Succession outcome (hardliner vs pragmatist) will determine whether Iran escalates further or seeks negotiated de-escalation. Hormuz closure duration is the key variable for global energy markets. No diplomatic resolution visible in the near term.
investor_implications: Iran is completely uninvestable. All existing exposures face total loss risk. Secondary sanctions apply to any entity transacting with Iran. The primary investment implication is the Hormuz closure's impact on global energy prices and Gulf state sovereigns. Iran crisis duration is the single most important variable for global commodity markets in Q1-Q2 2026.

active_alerts.title: Iran under massive US-Israeli military strikes; Supreme Leader Khamenei killed
active_alerts.description: US and Israel launched joint Operation Epic Fury on Feb 28, striking 1,000+ targets in Iran. Supreme Leader Khamenei was killed. Iran retaliated with strikes on 27 US bases and Gulf Arab states. IRGC consolidating control during 40-day mourning period. Succession crisis with no clear heir. Active military conflict continues with no ceasefire in sight. Conditions persist and may be worsening as IRGC pledges sustained retaliation campaign.

active_alerts.title: Iran economic collapse trajectory: strong_decrease across GDP, trade, FDI, current account
active_alerts.description: Iran shows strong_decrease trends in 8 of 11 tracked factors: GDP growth (strong_decrease), current account (strong_decrease), political stability (strong_decrease at -1.69), trade openness (strong_decrease), FDI (strong_decrease), exchange rate (strong_decrease), investment risk (strong_growth), and relationship health (strong_decrease). Inflation at 42.4% with strong_growth trend approaching hyperinflation. Iran is effectively uninvestable.

active_alerts.title: Iran inflation at 42.4% and rising sharply; approaching hyperinflation territory
active_alerts.description: Iran CPI inflation at 42.4% with strong_growth trend. Currency collapse, sanctions, military conflict, and Hormuz closure driving prices. Approaching the 50% hyperinflation threshold. Conditions persisting and worsening.
